person.name: Arnold de Bois
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
This Arnold or Ernald was one of the last in a long line which started in Normandy. By English counting, found for example in Dugdale, he was Ernald IV, but if we count the generations back in Normandy he is Ernald VII.

He died about 1 April1277.

Arnold died seised of the manor of Barfield in 1277, leaving a son and heir John, who held one knights fee of William Grimbald in Little Houghton and Brafield in 1284. This John died in 1295, his heir was his brother William.

In 1264, Ernald de Bosco held a part of the fee for the manor at Belgrave, Leicestershire, England; from whom it descended to his son John, who held it 1279-1280.

According to Smith he had the following children with his wife Alice, daughter of Henry de Tubney:

Arnold VIII de Bois, who married Ida, but died before his father. According to Smith it seems he died in a tournament.
John. 29 June 1253 - 1290) who married Joan.
Master William de Bois, a cleric.
Isabel, who married John Lovel of Titchmarsh
Joan, who married Roger Corbet of Hadleigh in Shropshire.
There was an inquisition made into his land holdings (an IPM) after he died.

sourceList.title: Our Royal, Titled, Noble, and Commoner Ancestors

zh.lifeSummary: 當Arnold de Bois於大約1222年出生在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾索普 阿諾德時，他的父親Ernald de Bois VI33歲，母親Joan Beauchamp31歲。他於1251年與Margery de Bois Of Claybrooke在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾可萊布盧可 帕瓦結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1277年4月1日在英國瓦維其可爾韋斯頓 武安德 維瑟利享年56歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bois
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Dubois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Pierre, Fernand, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Michel, Alain, Luc.

French, Walloon, and English (of French Huguenot origin): topographic name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ), or a habitational name for someone from any of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia) named Le Bois. This is the most frequent surname in Wallonia. It is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, and Haiti. In both England and North America it is sometimes translated into English as Wood . Compare Deboe , Debois , Deboy , Duboise , and Dubose .

History: This surname is listed (in the form Du Bois) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. One of the several Huguenot ancestors bearing this name was Chrétien Du Bois from Flanders, whose son, Louis Du Bois (born 1626 in Lille, France, died 1696 in Kingston, NY), was among the Huguenots who founded the town of New Paltz, NY 1675–78.

variants.name: Boisvert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Andre, Donat, Lucien, Jacques, Gaston, Normand, Pierre, Gilles, Herve, Adrien.
French:
topographic name for someone who lived in a dense forest or perhaps a copse of evergreens, from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + vert, verd ‘green’.

variants.name: Boisclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Marcel, Aldege, Alphonse, Arsene, Edmour, Emile, Leopaul, Prudent, Rejean, Remi, Yves.

French: topographic name from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + clair ‘clear, light’, or a habitational name from (Le) Bois Clair, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is virtually non-existing in France.

variants.name: Boies
meanings: Altered form of French   Bois  .

variants.name: Baskerville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Boscherville in Eure, France, named with Old Anglo-Norman French boschet ‘copse, thicket’ (a diminutive of Bois ) + ville ‘settlement, town’.

variants.name: Debois
meanings: Altered form of French   Dubois  , a cognate of 1 above.
French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ).

meanings: 1  Jewish: Yiddish contracted form of   Aaron .
2  English: short form of   Arnold .

historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Arnold de Bois was born about 1222, in Thorpe Arnold, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Ernald de Bois VI, was 33 and his mother, Joan Beauchamp, was 31. He married Margery de Bois Of Claybrooke in 1251, in Claybrooke Parva, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 1 April 1277, in Weston under Wetherley, Warwickshire, England, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Arnold de Bois est né environ 1222, à Thorpe Arnold, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Ernald de Bois VI, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Joan Beauchamp, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Margery de Bois Of Claybrooke en 1251, à Claybrooke Parva, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 1 avril 1277, à Weston under Wetherley, Warwickshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Arnold de Boisが約1222年にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアソルペ アルノルドで生まれた時，父Ernald de Bois VIは33歳，母Joan Beauchampは31歳であった。1251年にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアクレイブルーク パーヴァでMargery de Bois Of Claybrookeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1277年4月1日にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアウエストン ウンダー ウエスエルリーで56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Arnold de Bois(이)가 대략 1222년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 소 아노드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ernald de Bois VI(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Beauchamp(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1251년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 클레이브룩 파르바에서 Margery de Bois Of Claybrooke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1277년 4월 1일 영국 워윅실르 웨스턴 운더 w더리에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Margery de Bois Of Claybrooke em 1251, em Claybrooke Parva, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Arnold de Bois родился около 1222 г. в местности таурп арнолд, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Ernald de Bois VI было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Beauchamp, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Margery de Bois Of Claybrooke в 1251 г. в местности клеибрук парва, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 1 апреля 1277 г. в местности вестон ундир ветирли, варвиккшир, Англия в возрасте 56 лет.
